pi_items.source: Task description specifies exact filename and save path
Task specifies '45 degrees down from horizontal'. In standard CAD notation (0°=East, 90°=North, 270°=South), this is 315° or -45°. Verified: 315° ≡ -45° (mod 360°)
Task description: '36-degree beam angle (18 degrees on each side of the center line)'. Verified: 18 + 18 = 36
Calculated as center_angle + (beam_angle_total / 2) = 315 + 18 = 333. Verified by geometry: upper edge ray at 333° is consistent with +18° offset from center
Calculated as center_angle - (beam_angle_total / 2) = 315 - 18 = 297. Verified by geometry: lower edge ray at 297° is consistent with -18° offset from center

Independent geometric calculation: Ray from pipe at (2000, 5500) with upper ray at 333° hits floor at x=12794.36 mm; lower ray at 297° hits at x=4802.39 mm; width = 12794.36 - 4802.39 = 7991.97 mm (difference of 0.03 mm)
Specified in task.json metadata. Reasonable tolerance for 7992mm measurement is 50mm (±0.63%)
Specified in task.json metadata. Reasonable tolerance for angle measurements is 1 degree

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a LibreCAD drawing of a theatrical lighting setup with precise geometric specifications. All metadata values are mathematically consistent: the lighting pipe is positioned at (2000, 5500) mm with radius 24mm. The beam center line is angled at 315° (45° down from horizontal), with a 36° total spread creating upper and lower rays at 333° and 297° respectively. These rays originating from the pipe center reach the floor (y=0) at x-coordinates of approximately 12794 and 4802 mm respectively, creating a light pool width of 7992 mm (verified: 7991.97 mm). All angular and dimensional parameters are mathematically consistent and fall within specified tolerances.
